title: Enhanced autonomous uplink timing adjustment
abstract: A method for enhanced autonomous uplink timing adjustment includes determining that a user equipment (UE) is traveling above a predetermined velocity. In response to the determination that the UE is traveling above the predetermined velocity, a maximum aggregate adjustment rate to an uplink timing for the UE and a maximum amount of a magnitude of a timing change to the uplink timing for the UE are determined. The uplink timing for the UE is adjusted based on the maximum aggregate adjustment rate and the maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method, comprising; determining that a user equipment (UE) is traveling above a predetermined velocity; in response to the determination that the UE is traveling above the predetermined velocity: determining a maximum aggregate adjustment rate to an uplink timing for the UE; and determining a maximum amount of a magnitude of a timing change to the uplink timing for the UE; and adjusting the uplink timing for the UE based on the maximum aggregate adjustment rate and the maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein determining that the UE is traveling above a predetermined velocity is based on data from one or more sensors coupled to the UE. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the one or more sensors include at least one of an accelerometer, a gyroscope, or a global positioning system (GPS). 4. The method of claim 1, wherein determining that the UE is traveling above a predetermined velocity is based on an indication received by the UE from a network. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the indication from the network is a highSpeedEnhancedMeasFlag. 6. The method of claim 4, wherein the indication from the network is a SCCmeasurementEnhancements-rl 6 indication. 7. The method of claim 4, wherein the indication from the network is transmitted to the UE by radio resource control (RRC) signaling or medium access control (MAC) signaling. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein determining that the UE is traveling above a predetermined velocity is based on one or more uplink transmissions from the UE. 9. The method of claim 1, comprising transmitting, by the UE, an uplink transmission based on the uplink timing 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change comprises the maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change for a single adjustment to the uplink timing for the UE. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change is greater than 3.5 times a basic time unit for a network configured with a downlink bandwidth greater than or equal to 10 megahertz (MHz). 12. A user equipment (UE) device, comprising: one or more processors, and memory storing instructions that, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to perform operations comprising: determining that the UE is traveling above a predetermined velocity; in response to the determination that the UE is traveling above the predetermined velocity: determining a maximum aggregate adjustment rate to an uplink timing for the UE; and determining a maximum amount of a magnitude of a timing change to the uplink timing for the UE; and adjusting the uplink timing for the UE based on the maximum aggregate adjustment rate and the maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change. 13. The device of claim 12, wherein determining that the EE is traveling above a predetermined velocity is based on data from one or more sensors coupled to the UE. 14. The device of claim 12, wherein determining that the UE is traveling above a predetermined velocity is based on an indication received by the UE from a network. 15. The device of claim 14, wherein the indication from the network is a highSpeedEnhancedMeasFlag. 16. The device of claim 12, wherein the maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change is greater than 3.5 times a basic time unit for a network configured with a downlink bandwidth greater than or equal to 10 megahertz. (MHz) 17. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium storing instructions which, when executed by one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to perform operations comprising: determining that a user equipment (UE) is traveling above a predetermined velocity; in response to the determination that the HE is traveling above the predetermined velocity: determining a maximum aggregate adjustment rate to an uplink timing for the UE; and determining a maximum amount of a magnitude of a timing change to the uplink timing for the UE; and adjusting the uplink timing for the UE based on the maximum aggregate adjustment rate and the maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change. 18. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein determining that the UE is traveling above a predetermined velocity is based on data from one or more sensors coupled to the UE. 19. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein determining that the UE is traveling above a predetermined velocity is based on an indication received by the UE from a network. 20. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein the maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change is greater than 3.5 times 

 a basic time unit for a network configured with a downlink bandwidth greater than or equal to 10 megahertz (MHz).
